commit.message:
docs: fix curl examples to use GET and document HEAD pass-through behavior (#1699)

Manael skips conversion for non-`GET` requests (no response body to
read), so `curl -sI` (which sends `HEAD` by default) never triggers
conversion — confusing users who expect to see `Content-Type:
image/webp`.

## Changes

- **`curl` examples** — replace `curl -sI` with `curl -sI -X GET` in all
three docs files so examples actually exercise the conversion path
- **HEAD pass-through note** — add a callout in `README.md`,
`en/docs/usage/_index.md`, and `ja/docs/usage/_index.md` explicitly
stating that only `GET` requests are converted; `HEAD` and other methods
are proxied unchanged

```console
# Before (sends HEAD → no conversion)
curl -sI -H "Accept: image/webp" http://localhost:8080/image.jpg

# After (forces GET → conversion fires)
curl -sI -X GET -H "Accept: image/webp" http://localhost:8080/image.jpg
```

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> 
> ----
> 
> *This section details on the original issue you should resolve*
> 
> <issue_title>Docs: Update curl commands to use GET and document
pass-through behavior for HEAD requests</issue_title>
> <issue_description>### Background
> Currently, the documentation and README show examples using `curl -sI`
to test image conversion. However, `curl -I` sends a `HEAD` request by
default. Manael relies on reading the response body to determine
metadata (like file size and APNG detection) before performing the
conversion. Because `HEAD` requests do not return a body, Manael
intentionally skips conversion for `HEAD` requests (and any non-`GET`
methods) and passes the upstream response through unchanged.
> This can confuse users testing the proxy, as they won't see the
expected `Content-Type` changes.
> 
> ### Proposal
> 1. Update all `curl -sI` examples in the documentation to `curl -sI -X
GET`. This forces a `GET` request (triggering the conversion) while
still allowing curl to abort after reading the headers, keeping the
terminal output clean.
> 2. Add a clear note in the documentation explicitly stating that
Manael only processes `GET` requests, and that `HEAD` (and other HTTP
methods) are passed through to the upstream server without image
conversion.
> 
> ### Tasks
> - [ ] **Update `README.md`**: Change the `curl` example and add a
brief note about `GET` requests.
> - [ ] **Update `website/content/en/docs/usage/_index.md`**:
>   - Replace all instances of `curl -sI` with `curl -sI -X GET`.
>   - Add a note explaining that `HEAD` requests are not converted.
> - [ ] **Update `website/content/ja/docs/usage/_index.md`**:
>   - Replace all instances of `curl -sI` with `curl -sI -X GET`.
> - Add the corresponding note in Japanese explaining that `HEAD`
requests are not converted.
> 
> ### Example Note (English)
> > **Note:** Manael only converts images for `GET` requests. `HEAD`
requests (and other HTTP methods) are passed through to the upstream
server unchanged. When testing from the command line, use `curl -sI -X
GET` to fetch the headers of a converted image.
> 
> ### Example Note (Japanese)
> > **注意:** Manaelは `GET` リクエストに対してのみ画像変換を行います。`HEAD`
リクエスト（およびその他のHTTPメソッド）は変換されず、アップストリームのレスポンスがそのままクライアントへ返されます。コマンドラインからテストを行う場合は
`curl -sI -X GET` を使用してヘッダーを確認してください。</issue_description>
>

commit.message:
Clarify skillpm’s position relative to GitHub CLI/Claude plugin specs (#57)

Users asked what emerging GitHub CLI and Claude plugin specs imply for
skillpm. This PR clarifies that those specs are host/runtime extension
mechanisms, while skillpm remains focused on portable Agent Skills
packaging and orchestration.

- **Documentation: plugin-spec positioning**
  - Added a new section to `README.md`:
    - `How this relates to plugin specs (GitHub CLI / Claude Code)`
  - Added a matching section to `docs/index.md`:
    - `How this relates to plugin specs`
  - Both sections explicitly state:
    - plugin specs and Agent Skills are complementary, not replacements
- skillpm’s model is unchanged (`npm` for deps + `skills` for wiring +
`add-mcp` for MCP config)

- **Docs consistency**
- Kept language aligned across repository README and docs homepage so
users get the same answer regardless of entry point.

Example excerpt added:

```md
- Plugin specs define host-specific extension/runtime hooks.
- Agent Skills define portable instruction packages that can be reused across agent systems.

For skillpm, the model stays the same: npm for dependency management, plus `skills` and `add-mcp` for wiring and MCP configuration.
```

Detect self-contained projects in DotNetComponentDetector (#1689)

* Detect self-contained projects in DotNetComponentDetector

Report whether a .NET project target is self-contained by appending -selfcontained to the projectType (e.g. application-selfcontained).

Uses a heuristic based on the assets file: for each target framework, if any PackageDownload name starts with a FrameworkReference name followed by .Runtime, the target is considered self-contained. This covers both SelfContained=true and PublishAot=true scenarios, as both result in runtime package downloads in the assets file.

This relationship is not a guarantee.  The actual relationship is defined by the SDK's KnownFramework items, but we can't read those from build assets.  This convention has been followed for all in-support framework versions and should be acceptable.

* feat(network): add custom NetworkReceive and NetworkSend schedules (#202)

Replace NetworkSet::Receive/Send system sets in PreUpdate with dedicated
Bevy schedules inserted into MainScheduleOrder:

  PreUpdate > NetworkReceive > StateTransition > ... > PostUpdate > NetworkSend

NetworkReceive drains inbound async events and runs module reaction
systems (on-connect sends, orchestration, stream handlers). NetworkSend
runs outbound broadcasts after all gameplay and PostUpdate mutations.

Within NetworkReceive, NetworkSet::Drain and NetworkSet::Commands provide
intra-schedule ordering; module systems run between them by default.

Broadcast systems (broadcast_state, broadcast_gas_grid,
broadcast_item_event) moved from Update to NetworkSend so clients always
receive fully-committed world state.

* Fix TimeEditor cursor/insertion by normalizing time format patterns

Single-digit format specifiers (h, H, m, s) caused variable-width
fields where cursor positions shifted based on the current time value.
Typing "12" at position 0 of a single-digit hour would place "1" in
hours and "2" in minutes because CursorRight skipped the separator
at position 1.

Normalize all format specifiers to 2-digit versions (hh, HH, mm, ss)
so field positions are always consistent. Remove the leading space
hack from DisplayText since padding is no longer needed.

Add 9 unit tests covering cursor navigation, insertion at all digit
positions, separator skipping, and DriverAssert rendering verification.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Improve TimeEditor width and TextValidateField cursor logic

- TimeEditor now sets width to fit full DisplayText (incl. AM/PM).
- TextValidateField allows cursor one past last editable char.
- Fixed width calculation for fixed fields (DisplayText.Length + 1).
- Updated tests for new width and cursor behavior.
- Added labels in TimeAndDate demo to show time patterns.
- Added tests to verify full AM/PM visibility and width logic.
- Improves usability and prevents clipping of time strings.

* Improve cursor handling at end of masked text fields

Enhances navigation and editing in masked fields (TextValidateField, TimeEditor) to prevent the cursor from moving backward when pressing right arrow from the blank cell past the last editable character. Backspace from the blank cell now deletes the last editable character. TimeEditor minimum width is increased by one to always allow a blank cell. Updates logic for right-arrow navigation in fixed providers. Adds unit tests to verify correct cursor and editing behavior at the end of input. These changes address subtle usability issues and make cursor movement more intuitive.

* Replace TimeField with new TimeEditor control

Removed the TimeField control, its tests, and documentation. Introduced TimeEditor as the new time input control, based on TextValidateField with culture-aware formatting. Updated all references, scenarios, and docs to use TimeEditor instead of TimeField. TimeField source and tests have been deleted.

* Refactor TimeEditor and providers, remove VerifyChar method

Refactored TimeEditor, TimeTextProvider, NetMaskedTextProvider, and TextRegexProvider for clarity and consistency. Removed the VerifyChar method from ITextValidateProvider and all implementations, simplifying the provider interface. Standardized event raising and improved handling of AM/PM, manual parsing, and pattern normalization in TimeTextProvider. Updated TextValidateField drawing and key handling logic for simplicity. Modernized and clarified all related tests to match the new API and style. Made various code style improvements and updated documentation throughout.

* Implement CWP and IValue<T> for TextValidateField/TimeEditor

Refactored TextValidateField and TimeEditor to fully implement the IValue<T> interface and follow the Cancellable Work Pattern (CWP) for value changes.
- TextValidateField now exposes Value, ValueChanging, and ValueChanged events, and synchronizes value changes from both programmatic and user input, with support for cancellation and reversion.
- Introduced SuppressValueEvents to prevent unwanted event recursion.
- TimeEditor now uses a private _value field, leverages CWPPropertyHelper for value changes, and raises strongly-typed TimeSpan events.
- Removed manual event wiring and last-known-value tracking in favor of the new base class pattern.
- These changes improve consistency, extensibility, and support for data binding and undo/redo scenarios.

* Replace DateField with new DateEditor based on TextValidateField

Add DateTextProvider (ITextValidateProvider for dates with culture-aware
formatting, pattern normalization, separator handling) and DateEditor
(extends TextValidateField, implements IValue<DateTime?> with CWP).
Remove legacy DateField and update all usages in DatePicker, scenarios,
docs, and indexes. Full test coverage in DateEditorTests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Improved null safety in TextRegexProvider by initializing _text to an empty list. Fixed CleanSeparator logic in DateTextProvider for short separators. commit.message:
⚡ Bolt: Resolve N+1 query bottleneck in Search Service (#115)

commit.message:
fix(esoteric): R4 deep audit — VoC 360° edge normalization, hot-path influence skip, O(1) planet name array

VoC: normalize moonPos to [0,360) before sign computation — (int)(360.0/30.0)=12
produces invalid zodiac sign index and signEnd=390, breaking the applying-aspect
range check at the Pisces/Aries boundary (false VoC positive -> -5.0 penalty).

Hot-path: gate PlanetaryInfluences init (7 writes), per-aspect CollectionsMarshal
mutations (~24/call), name lookups (~24/call), and Mercury/VoC influence writes
behind computeInterpretation flag. Eliminates ~5.3M dict ops per 220K-candle
backtest on the EsotericFeatureProvider hot path.

Planet names: replace PlanetNames.GetValueOrDefault FrozenDictionary hash probes
with flat PlanetNamesByIdFast[planetId] array (O(1) indexing) for cold-path
usages (interpretation, debug logging).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
docs(*): add bootstrap step to `copilot-instructions.md` (#517)

The Copilot instructions did not tell agents to bootstrap the workspace
before running validation or making package changes. In a fresh clone,
that leads to avoidable failures and wasted setup time.

- **Summary**
- Add explicit bootstrap guidance to `.github/copilot-instructions.md`.
- Place it directly before the existing test/build/lint workflow so the
install requirement is seen before any validation commands.

- **Changes**
  - Introduce a new `Bootstrapping` section in the Copilot instructions.
- Require `npm install` from the repo root before tests, builds, lint,
or implementation work.
- Clarify that work should not start until install completes
successfully because workspace dependencies are shared across the
monorepo.

- **Resulting guidance**
  ```md
  ## Bootstrapping

- In a fresh clone, run `npm install` from the repo root before
attempting tests, builds, lint, or package changes.
- Do not start validation or implementation work until the install has
completed successfully, because the workspace dependencies are required
across the monorepo.
  ```

fix(web): R4 deep audit — circuit memory leaks on InstrumentePage/FeatureImportancePage, wire CTS to TopologiePage grain calls

InstrumentePage: Dispose now releases _assetsByCategory, _allAssets, _categories,
_rules, _activePortfolio, _discoveryResult — previously only set _disposed flag
while all data stayed pinned to circuit memory after navigation.

FeatureImportancePage: Added IDisposable to release _result (70+ feature entries)
on navigation. Previously had no disposal at all.

TopologiePage: Wired _cts.Token into Task.WhenAll via WaitAsync so DisposeAsync
actually cancels in-flight Orleans grain RPCs instead of waiting for completion
on a dead component.

commit.message:
Switch to Flex Consumption plan with blob-based deployment

Following the official MS quickstart template for Flex Consumption
with managed identities:
- Flex Consumption plan (FC1) uses blob container for deployment
  instead of Azure Files — no file share or shared key access needed
- Storage account: allowSharedKeyAccess=false (policy-compliant)
- User-assigned managed identity with Blob Data Owner/Contributor,
  Queue Data Contributor, Table Data Contributor roles
- functionAppConfig with blobContainer deployment storage
- App settings use AzureWebJobsStorage__accountName/credential/clientId
- No WEBSITE_CONTENTAZUREFILECONNECTIONSTRING needed
